question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R4 (PE2) marks GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R4↔R3 link) as OSPF passive.
Passive-enable stops Hello transmission/reception on the interface;
the OSPF adjacency to R3 drops. R4 still has its R4-R7 OSPF link (Area
0) as an alternate path to R3's loopback, so iBGP to R3 stays Up via
the R4→R7→R3 detour — but the R4-R3 direct link is no longer in the
SPF tree.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.104 (R4, PE2), GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R4<->R3 link) was set OSPF passive (passive enable). Passive mode suppresses OSPF Hello packets on the interface, so the two ends can no longer exchange Hellos and the OSPF adjacency on the R3<->R4 direct link cannot be maintained; that link drops out of the SPF tree.
Because R4 retains its area-0 link to R7 as an alternate path to R3's loopback, reachability to R3's loopback is preserved via the R4->R7->R3 detour, so the iBGP session between R3 and R4 stays Up over the alternate path.
Fix: remove passive (no passive) on R4's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.3.

OSPF adjacency state change (%ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "interface down or detached".
- Bilateral: both ends (R3, R4) report the transition.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 passive enable

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no passive

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:31:19.977 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Down - RR client configuration changed (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:31:24.746 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:31:19.978 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.3 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:31:57.018 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.4 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:31:22.574 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: interface down or detached, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000
